content: For a tokenized portfolio with many investors holding proportional exposure, ERC-20 is usually the simpler fit. ERC-721 is better when each token represents a unique asset or investor position. The PRD should also capture the 50-wallet whitelist, allocation validation, valuation/performance update requirements, and testnet-only wallet-signed deployment gate.

suggestedRequirementUpdates.proposedValue: ERC-20 preferred unless unique investor positions are required
suggestedRequirementUpdates.rationale: The MVP describes proportional portfolio exposure and distribution to up to 50 wallets.

suggestedRequirementUpdates.proposedValue: up to 50 whitelisted investor wallet addresses
suggestedRequirementUpdates.rationale: The current MVP scale is one asset manager and up to 50 investor wallets.

openQuestions: Should investors hold fungible shares or unique token positions?
What valuation file fields will be uploaded daily?

protocolComparison.erc20: Best for fungible portfolio shares, percentage allocations, mint/distribute flows, and familiar token-holder balances.
protocolComparison.erc721: Best for unique positions, individually identified assets, or non-fungible claims where each token has distinct meaning.
protocolComparison.recommendation: Start from ERC-20 for the MVP unless the asset manager needs each investor position to be unique.

riskNotes: This is product-engineering guidance, not legal, investment, or formal audit advice.
Generated Solidity remains demo/testnet scaffold until compiled, tested, QA-reviewed, and security-reviewed.
nextRecommendedAction: Confirm fungible versus unique-token behavior before PRD approval.
